explanations.8: This tells VS Code where to find the phpcbf tool (a PHP code formatter) on your computer — it's an absolute file path to the executable.
explanations.10: This tells VS Code where to find the phpcs tool (a PHP code sniffer that detects style violations) — similar to line 8 but for a different tool.
explanations.12: This enables the fixer, meaning VS Code will automatically try to correct PHP code style issues when you save or request a fix.
explanations.14: When phpcs finds style problems, this setting makes it show you the name/source of which rule was violated, not just the error.
explanations.16: This points to a configuration file that defines which PHP coding standard (like PSR-12) your project should follow.
explanations.22: This installs the phpsab extension into VS Code, which is the plugin that uses those phpcs and phpcbf tools you configured above.
